response.justification: The role carries a hard minimum of 8 years C++ programming experience; this candidate's resume shows at most six months of C++ work at National Instruments (late 2014 – mid 2015) plus a bachelor-thesis side project, and C++ does not appear in the skills section at all. Beyond that critical language gap, the candidate has no formal technical leadership or cloud-platform ownership history, and their primary stack (TypeScript, Node.js, D-lang) is entirely misaligned with the systems-level, cloud-compute-platform context the role demands.
strengths: Hands-on exposure to genuinely high-scale distributed systems at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby — millions of HTTP requests per second — demonstrating comfort with low-latency, performance-critical infrastructure
Consistent TDD advocacy and a track record of teaching engineering best practices through workshops, code katas, and refactoring dojos across multiple roles
Demonstrated ability to own and ship end-to-end open-source infrastructure (GISCollective, Kubernetes-deployed, used in production by Green Map) showing project-level technical autonomy
concerns: C++ experience is critically absent — the role requires 8 years minimum and the resume shows roughly 6 months; the language is not even listed in the candidate's skills
No formal technical leadership or team-mentoring role in a cloud or DevOps context; workshop facilitation and informal dojos do not satisfy the 2-year cloud/DevOps leadership requirement
Cloud architecture depth is superficial — AWS CDK usage for a pricing feature and a personal Kubernetes deployment do not constitute production-level cloud compute platform design or ownership
